# Postcraft — Foundation Research (auto-generated)

> Generated by the postcraft-foundation-research workflow (7 agents, ~576K tokens). Source of truth for build decisions.

I'll synthesize the six research streams into the decision-grade report.

# AI Company-to-Carousel Engine — Decision-Grade Build Report

## 1. Executive Summary

Build a **Portuguese-native B2B content engine** that ingests a company's full public footprint (website, news, owned social, named competitors), synthesizes an **editable ICP object**, and generates **ICP-tuned visual posts and carousels** where the design craft — not the ingestion — is the headline differentiator. The market splits into four camps that each own one piece: ad-creative engines (AdCreative.ai), generic social generators (Predis.ai), carousel specialists (PostNitro), and writing suites (Jasper). None fuse auto-ingestion + true ICP modeling + per-slide carousel craft + competitor visual research into one loop. Auto-ingestion alone is no longer novel (SocialPost.ai already does website-to-content), so the moat is the **system**: company-intelligence → editable ICP → competitor visual playbook → deterministic per-slide design. Wedge on **B2B agencies** (highest willingness to pay) priced per-brand-workspace + credits, anchored against AdCreative ($39/$249), not $19 schedulers. Beachhead: BR B2B commerce/distribution (the BR B2B vertical), billed in BRL via Pix Automático.

## 2. Competitive Landscape & the 3 Differentiation Wedges We Own

**The field splits into four camps, none owning all three pieces:**

| Camp | Leaders | Owns | Misses |
|------|---------|------|--------|
| Paid-ad creative engines | AdCreative.ai ($39–$599) | Competitor intel, creative scoring (340 data points, trained on $35B+ ad spend) | Templated/interchangeable output, no carousel craft, no ICP storytelling |
| Organic social generators | Predis.ai ($19/$40/$212), Ocoya, SocialPost.ai | Carousels + brand kit; SocialPost.ai does website-to-content auto-extraction | Shallow visual quality, industry-vertical (not granular ICP) targeting |
| Carousel specialists | PostNitro ($10), ContentDrips ($15–26), Draft AI, Taplio | Best layout discipline + brand-kit consistency | No company-intelligence ingestion — pure execution, no intelligence layer |
| Writing/brand suites | Jasper ($39), Copy.ai ($49), Typeface (enterprise) | Brand voice from website (Copy.ai) | Visuals are an afterthought |

Two axes are weak across the **entire field**: genuine ICP targeting (tools at best let you "pick an audience") and per-slide carousel craft (incumbents stamp one template N times — AdCreative is "templated/interchangeable"; Predis has "no detail/control over image quality"). This matches Luigi's own field note: 6 same-structure slides = "6 capas em loop." (sources: adcreative.ai, predis.ai/resources, socialpost.ai newswire, getdraft.io, toolsforhumans.ai)

**The 3 wedges we own (the loop, not single features):**

1. **Company-intelligence → editable ICP graph as the engine.** Auto-crawl site + news + owned social + 2–3 named competitors, then synthesize an explicit, *visible, steerable* ICP object (pains, vocabulary, objections, buying triggers, proof points). Incumbents stop at "brand colors/voice" (SocialPost.ai) or "pick an audience" (AdCreative) — none expose a real, editable ICP that re-targets every post.

2. **Per-slide carousel art direction as the craft moat.** Treat a carousel as a designed narrative with *different anatomy per slide* (cover opens the theme; middles each carry one stat/quote/list; closing CTA). Solve the text-in-image + hierarchy problems AdCreative/Predis are criticized for. Hardest to copy → headline differentiator.

3. **Competitor/reference visual research fused INTO generation.** Crawl category leaders' + named competitors' actual visual social output, distill the design language (palette, type, layout patterns, hook styles), use it to art-direct ICP-tuned carousels, and show the reference alongside the output for trust. Today this lives in disconnected inspo tools (Inspo AI, Savee) or competitor-AD galleries (AdCreative) — nobody closes the loop. (sources: inspoai.io, creativebloq.com)

**BR opening:** GalilAI and Postig are thin, template-driven, with no ICP or auto-ingestion; PostNitro offers PT-BR but is a global template tool. A Portuguese-first, ICP-aware entrant has a defensible local opening before global players localize depth. (sources: galilai.com.br, postig.com.br, postnitro.ai/pt)

## 3. Recommended Technical Stack (Provider-Agnostic)

Enforce a **normalized `CompanyProfile` schema** (`identity, products[], brandKit{logo,colors[],fonts[],spacing}, social{platform,posts[],engagement}, news[], voiceBrief`) so every adapter maps INTO it and the content engine never touches a raw provider response. Each adapter = one interface (fetch → normalize). This prevents vendor lock-in (enforce the boundary in code review).

### 3a. Company-Intelligence Extraction

| Adapter | Primary | Fallback / Swap | Cost notes |
|---------|---------|-----------------|------------|
| `CrawlSource` | Firecrawl `/map` (discover /about /products /pricing /blog) → `/scrape` (markdown) → `/extract` (JSON schema for hard facts) | — | 1 credit/page; **AI extract = 5 credits/req** — prefer plain scrape + own LLM extraction; gate extract behind a quota |
| `BrandSource` | Firecrawl `branding` format (logo, hex palette, fonts, spacing in one scrape call) | node-vibrant/ColorThief + `@font-face`/Wappalyzer; Brandprint.io if decoupled | Cheapest path when already crawling |
| `SocialSource` | Apify: `apify/instagram-scraper` ($1.50/1k), `harvestapi/linkedin-company-posts` (~$2/1k, no cookies), `clockworks/tiktok-profile-scraper` ($0.30/1k) | ≥1 alternate actor per network in config | Per-company full profile lands ~$0.50–$2 |
| `NewsSource` | GDELT DOC 2.0 + Google News RSS (free breadth) | NewsData.io ($199.99/mo) for commercial structured/full-text | Free feeds are undocumented/unstable — budget paid for production |
| `VoiceSynthesizer` | LLM step over scraped corpus → structured one-page brand-voice brief (traits, vocabulary rules, banned phrases, formatting, sentiment arc) | — | Privilege **high-engagement posts** (real voice) over website boilerplate (aspirational voice) |

Start on Firecrawl Hobby ($16) + Apify Free/Starter ($29). **Cache and content-hash everything** (raw HTML, screenshots, datasets) so re-runs are cheap and voice/brand-kit synthesis only re-fires when source content changes. Gate every Apify actor with a 10-item pilot before backfill to absorb IG/LinkedIn anti-bot drift. (sources: firecrawl.dev/pricing, firecrawl.dev/blog/branding-format-v2, apify.com/apify/instagram-scraper, apify.com/harvestapi/linkedin-company-posts, blog.gdeltproject.org)

### 3b. Image + Carousel Generation — VERDICT: Hybrid AI-Texture + Deterministic-Text

**The research strongly VALIDATES the hybrid thesis. Adopt it as default.** Diffusion models process prompts as visual patterns, not linguistic structure, so they depict text-*like* shapes rather than spell reliably. Even best-in-class (Ideogram 3.0) tops out at ~90–95% on **short** strings and degrades fast on paragraphs, exact fonts, kerning, and brand hex; Imagen 4 explicitly recommends <25 chars/phrase. Embedding load-bearing copy in generation is a quality and consistency liability across 6–10 slides. (sources: makeuseof.com, mindstudio.ai/blog/what-is-ideogram-v3, developers.googleblog.com)

**Two-layer architecture:**

- **AI generates the background/texture/hero ONLY (text-free prompts).** Route through **fal.ai** as the single aggregator. Default **FLUX.2 [dev/Turbo]** for photoreal/abstract B2B textures (~$0.008–0.012/MP, accepts exact brand hex). **Recraft V3** ($0.04 raster / $0.08 vector, #1 on text-to-image arena) for clean vector/icon/flat graphics. Reserve **Ideogram 3.0 / Seedream 4.x** only for rare short decorative text accents baked into a hero — never body copy. **Drop Midjourney** — no usable official API; unofficial wrappers risk bans. (sources: fal.ai/pricing, fal.ai/models/fal-ai/flux-2-pro, recraft.ai/blog, 10b.ai/blog/does-midjourney-have-an-api)
- **A deterministic renderer composites ALL text.** Standardize on an HTML/CSS template rendered at exactly **1080×1350 with deviceScaleFactor=2** (2160×2700 effective) for retina-sharp type. Use **Playwright** for full CSS (grid, complex effects, web fonts) — matches the proven the proven HTML→PNG pipeline. Use **Satori + resvg** only if moving to serverless/edge (flexbox-only, no `display:grid`). **#1 silent failure: fonts** — embed (base64/local) and `await` font load before screenshot. (sources: github.com/vercel/satori, contentful.com/blog/puppeteer-node-open-graph)

Buy-vs-build for the template layer: Templated.io ($29/mo, 1k renders, Canva import) or Placid ($15–39/mo) if avoiding Playwright infra. Canva Connect API is gated behind Enterprise — skip unless already there. For Luigi's existing self-hosted pipeline, **stay on HTML→PNG** — cheaper and more controllable. (sources: bannerbear.com/pricing, canva.dev/docs/connect)

### 3c. Visual Reference Research — 5-Stage Module

**DEFINE → DISCOVER → COLLECT → EXTRACT → ENCODE**, with **separate boards for GLOBAL (craft ceiling) vs LOCAL (market context)** so mediocre local norms never dilute the aspirational ceiling.

- **DISCOVER:** GLOBAL via Behance/Dribbble/Savee/Cosmos + TikTok Creative Center + Foreplay (200M+ ad DB, carousel/format filter); LOCAL via Meta Ad Library (advertiser-name search) + IG/LinkedIn handle & geo-hashtag search.
- **COLLECT:** Apify `facebook-ads-scraper` (~$0.65/1k) + `instagram-scraper-pro` (multi-handle competitor mode); Firecrawl schema-scrape for one-off pages. Meta has **no public creative API** — web-UI scraping required.
- **EXTRACT/RANK:** Weighted engagement score — **saves ×3, shares ×4, comments ×2, likes ×0.5**, normalized per-follower. For paid ads where engagement isn't visible, substitute **run-duration ≥30d + variation-density ≥3** ("one winner is chance; five is a system"). The Meta "started running" date is the single best free performance proxy.
- **ENCODE:** Collapse top-N into a versioned **visual playbook** = JSON design tokens (ONE primary + neutral + ONE accent; one display + one body font) + prompt-rules block (slide template per content type, cover formula, hook taxonomy, layout grammar). Run a Claude-vision pass to extract semantic color/type roles. **Rule: steal the structure, not the words/assets** (IP/ToS guardrail). Refresh LOCAL weekly, GLOBAL monthly. (sources: superscale.ai/learn/competitor-ads, foreplay.co/swipe-file, sproutsocial.com/insights/instagram-engagement-rate, postnitro.ai/blog/post/carousel-by-design)

**Caveat to state explicitly:** saves/shares/reach are NOT publicly exposed, so any organic ranking is approximated from likes+comments+views — treat rankings as directional.

## 4. The ICP → Pillars → Post Concept → Carousel Spec Method

**Step 1 — Derive a 5-layer ICP (not just firmographics).** The scraper fills: (1) Firmographics (industry, size, revenue, geo, funding); (2) Technographics (CRM/tooling, competitive stack); (3) Behavioral signals (hiring, funding events, content engagement); (4) Organizational readiness (decision-maker title, team size, process maturity); (5) **Negative indicators** — require an explicit "negative ICP" field so the pipeline can disqualify, not just qualify. If won/lost deals share identical firmographics, the ICP lacks dimensionality. (source: landbase.com/blog/how-to-define-icp-b2b-framework-2026)

**Step 2 — Expand into a B2B buying-committee matrix.** Layer JTBD (functional/emotional/social) onto 5 archetypes, each consuming different content: **Champion** (ROI + peer case studies), **Economic Buyer** (TCO/payback, NOT features), **Technical Evaluator** (architecture/security/integrations), **End User** (demos/workflow fit), **Blocker/Skeptic** (evidence not enthusiasm — the role where most deals die). Each cell = one content thread; prevents marketing only to the champion. (sources: velocityengine.co, tractioncomplete.com)

**Step 3 — Generate 3 content pillars as authority claims, not topic buckets.** Discover 5–7, narrow to 3 validated. Each sits at the intersection of (genuine expertise) × (obsessive audience pain) × (untaken angle). Express as "I'm-the-person-who…" statements and pass the **name-removal test** (could 5 others write this identically?). Score each angle 1–10 on audience obsession; **kill anything below 6**; tag painkiller vs vitamin in the audience's own language. (source: promptsdaily.substack.com)

**Step 4 — Fan each pillar into 5 post concepts** from the 5 lenses: **Educator, Contrarian, Storyteller, Tactician, Vulnerable** — testing signal diversity, not one safe take.

**Step 5 — Lock the carousel spec** (the differentiated, per-slide anatomy):
- **Cover (hook):** ≤8–10 words, one central idea, readable in <2s, answers "is this for me?" + "what do I get?"; pattern interrupt (high-contrast / expressive face / dramatic graphic) + curiosity trigger + directional cue; **made visually incomplete** (cropped diagram / partial checklist) to force the swipe.
- **Slide 2:** skepticism-reducer (why-this-matters / roadmap / proof / self-qualifying question).
- **Slides 3–9:** one idea per slide, ≤12 words, flashcard style, chained with open loops ("Next: the fix that takes 2 minutes"). Penultimate = **proof slide** (named numbers, before/after, case data).
- **Final:** dual CTA — one soft mid-carousel, one explicit close. **Decide saves-vs-shares up front:** checklist/framework format + "save this" for saves; "send this to whoever handles X" for shares.
- Narrative = 5-beat: **Hook → Context → Discovery → Reward → CTA.** 8–10 slides IG / 7–10 LinkedIn, 1080×1350. (sources: truefuturemedia.com, marketingagent.blog, postnitro.ai)

**Division of labor:** AI handles mechanics (structure, first-draft slide copy, layout consistency, 3–5 hook variants); **a human picks/sharpens the hook and injects specific named numbers before ship** — this is the load-bearing differentiator that prevents generic template output. Maintain a Winners/Losers archive + hook swipe file as persistent memory in the vault so the pipeline compounds. (source: designlumo.com)

> Note: scraped data captures firmographics/technographics well but is weak on real intent/JTBD/committee composition — the framework expects 5–10 buyer interviews to validate. Over-trusting scraped-only ICPs produces confident-but-wrong personas. Most engagement benchmarks (68% win-rate lift, 5–10% save rate, 1.9× reach) are vendor blogs — treat as directional and recalibrate per account from its own baseline.

## 5. Pricing, Packaging & the 0→1 Wedge

**Wedge:** Lead with **B2B marketing agencies** (and fractional/solo SMMs serving B2B) as the *revenue* wedge — highest willingness to pay (they resell content at $1.5K–$50K/mo), need multi-brand + whitelabel, and validate the per-brand pricing axis. In-house B2B marketers are the *product-fit* wedge. SMBs/solopreneurs are too price-sensitive to anchor 0→1. **One sharp beachhead first: BR B2B commerce/distribution (the BR B2B vertical).** (sources: almcorp.com, frase.io/blog)

**Pricing model = hybrid: per-brand-workspace base + usage credits** (85% of companies use usage pricing; credit models grew 126% YoY, but pure usage exposes you to volatile inference costs — the base fee must cover fixed cost so credits are pure margin). Anchor against **AdCreative ($39/$249/$599) and Jasper ($39 Creator)**, NOT $19 schedulers. (sources: data-mania.com, metronome.com, bvp.com/atlas)

| Tier | Global price | Brands | Credits | Gated value amplifiers |
|------|-------------|--------|---------|------------------------|
| Free | $0 | 1 | ~50/mo | (core aha only, watermarked) |
| Solo/Pro | **$39–$49/mo** | 1–2 | ~150–200 | un-watermarked, brand-voice memory |
| Agency | **$199–$299/mo** | up to ~10 | ~1,000 | whitelabel, client folders, bulk gen |
| Agency Scale/Enterprise | custom | unlimited | — | API, seats, dedicated |

Sell credit top-ups (~+1,200 credits / $29). **BR pricing:** Portuguese-native UI/onboarding/prompts from day one (68% of BR buyers prefer native-language product); price in BRL with ~40–50% PPP adjustment (global $49 → **~R$99–R$149/mo**); bill via **Pix Automático** (near-zero fee vs 4–7% cards, ~30% conversion lift). **Geo-gate Pix/BRL to BR** (IP/billing-country checks) to prevent arbitrage. (sources: ebanx.com, gartner.com/en/digital-markets, dodopayments.com/blogs/pix-payments, stripe.com/en-br/resources)

**Packaging = reverse trial:** 7–14 days full access (all ICP intelligence, all formats, un-watermarked, all brands) auto-downgrading to the capped free tier. ~65% of PLG SaaS use hybrid; it outperforms pure freemium and pure trial. **Gate value amplifiers only (multi-brand, whitelabel, high-res export, brand/ICP memory, bulk, competitor analysis, API) — NEVER gate the core aha.** (sources: thoughtlytics.com, dodopayments.com/blogs/saas-free-trial-vs-freemium)

**Activation aha (north-star = time-to-first-exported-asset):** paste URL + describe ICP → complete on-brand, ICP-targeted asset set in **<5 minutes**, no manual brand setup. Every 10-min delay to first value costs ~8% conversion; trials convert 15–25% vs 2–5% freemium. (sources: thegood.com, ordwaylabs.com)

**0→1 GTM = founder-led build-in-public + product-as-distribution, NOT paid ads.** Dogfood the engine on your own brand publicly (generate its ICP content as ongoing proof); put a subtle "made with [product]" footer on free-tier exports (every shared asset = a distribution loop); land first 10–20 paying agencies via warm BR B2B outreach → case studies → open self-serve. (sources: ainativegtm.substack.com, definerbrands.com)

**Defensibility:** make the **per-brand ICP + brand-knowledge memory the moat** — the longer a customer's ICP lives in the system and learns from outputs, the higher the switching cost. Competitors copy a feature, not your accumulated ICP knowledge graph. (source: m1-project.com)

## 6. Top 7 Risks & How Product Design Mitigates Each

1. **Auto-ingestion is no longer novel (SocialPost.ai, Copy.ai, Jasper, Typeface all do website analysis).** → Don't sell ingestion. Make the *editable ICP + per-slide carousel craft + competitor visual research loop* the product spine; ingestion is plumbing, not pitch.

2. **Priced-low commodity floor (Canva $14.99, Predis $19–$32).** → Position B2B/brand-grade, anchor to AdCreative/Jasper, and lead messaging with "Not another template stamper" — design craft + transparent pricing + an editable ICP, the three axes leaders are weakest on. Escape the commodity floor by category, not by price.

3. **Carousel craft is genuinely hard — it's exactly where every incumbent fails.** → Hybrid AI-texture + deterministic-text architecture: never embed load-bearing copy in generation; HTML→PNG at 2× scale guarantees pixel-perfect brand typography. Different anatomy per slide enforced by the spec template. Don't underestimate the design/eng effort here — it's the moat.

4. **Bad ingestion → off-brand/factually-wrong posts (reputationally fatal).** → Human-in-the-loop verification: extracted brand kit + ICP are *drafts*, not ground truth; keep screenshot + manual-override paths; require a human to confirm ICP/brand facts and inject named numbers before generation. Cache + content-hash so re-runs only re-fire on real source change.

5. **Scraping ToS/IP + platform anti-bot volatility (IG/LinkedIn/Meta no public creative API; GDPR/LGPD).** → Restrict to public company pages; document lawful basis; avoid storing commenters' personal data. Rule: "steal the structure, not the words/assets." Per-network adapter config with ≥1 alternate actor + 10-item pilots + Issues-tab monitoring absorb breakage.

6. **Crowded, feature-converging market — a thin differentiator is copied in one release cycle.** → Ship the *system* (ingestion → ICP → competitor visual research → per-slide craft as one loop) + accumulating per-brand ICP memory as switching cost. Speed-to-localized-BR-niche is the durable defense a solo founder can hold vs AdCreative/Jasper out-resourcing a single feature.

7. **PPP/BRL arbitrage + narrow-beachhead TAM risk + founder-time concentration.** → Geo-gate Pix/BRL tier to BR (IP/billing checks). Prove the BR B2B vertical, then validate expansion adjacency early before TAM assumptions calcify. Engineer distribution into the product (free-tier footer loop) so growth isn't fully bottlenecked on founder audience.

## 7. Prioritized MVP Feature List (must-have to charge money)

**P0 — the chargeable core loop:**
1. **URL/brand-kit ingest → auto brand profile** (Firecrawl `/map` + `/scrape` + `branding` format; node-vibrant fallback). Output the normalized `CompanyProfile`.
2. **ICP definition — guided + EDITABLE** (5-layer model with a required negative-ICP field; the visible, steerable artifact that is the differentiator).
3. **Coherent multi-asset generation tuned to that ICP** — one carousel (per-slide anatomy spec) + captions + 2–3 visual/ad variants, in the brand's voice. Hybrid: fal.ai (FLUX.2/Recraft) text-free backgrounds + Playwright HTML→PNG at 1080×1350 @2× for all text.
4. **Export** with quality/watermark gated by tier.
5. **Per-brand workspace + credit metering** (the pricing axis).
6. **Pix Automático + Stripe billing** with BRL/PPP pricing geo-gated to BR.
7. **<5-min activation flow** instrumenting time-to-first-exported-asset.

**P1 — the wedge enablers (sequence right AFTER first paying agencies, not before):**
8. Multi-brand workspaces + whitelabel/remove-branding (agency revenue wedge).
9. Persistent brand-voice/ICP memory (the moat).
10. Competitor visual reference research module (5-stage; GLOBAL vs LOCAL boards) feeding the playbook — the third wedge; can launch as "lite" (Meta Ad Library + IG handle scrape + Claude-vision token extraction).
11. Reverse-trial mechanics (7–14d full → capped free auto-downgrade) + free-tier "made with" footer loop.
12. Bulk/batch generation; hook A/B variants; Winners/Losers archive in the vault.

**Explicitly DEFER until the content loop is proven and paid:** scheduling, analytics dashboards, deep competitor scraping at scale, video, API, multi-seat. (source: m1-project.com on the content-generation loop as the spine to prove first)
